items.question: What is the main purpose of data augmentation as regularization?
options: Increase model parameter count
Expand effective training diversity with label-preserving transforms
Replace the need for a validation set
Eliminate all label noise in the dataset
items.answer: Expand effective training diversity with label-preserving transforms
items.explanation: Augmentation creates varied training views without new labels, reducing overfitting to exact training rows.

items.question: Which train/val pattern suggests augmentation may help?
options: High training error and high validation error
Low training error and much higher validation error
Zero training and validation error
High training error and low validation error
items.answer: Low training error and much higher validation error
items.explanation: A large train–val gap indicates overfitting (variance); augmentation is a variance reducer.

items.question: Why avoid random heavy augmentation on the validation set?
options: It slows GPU inference
It makes metrics noisy and not comparable to deployment inputs
It violates GDPR
It prevents batch normalization from working
items.answer: It makes metrics noisy and not comparable to deployment inputs
items.explanation: Validation should use stable preprocessing so you measure true generalization.

items.question: Horizontal flip is a bad default augmentation for which task?
options: Natural photo cat vs dog classification
Left vs right arrow direction classification
Satellite land vs water (symmetric classes)
Random object in COCO-style detection with symmetric objects
items.answer: Left vs right arrow direction classification
items.explanation: Mirroring swaps left and right — the label changes, so flip is not label-preserving.

items.question: SMOTE should be applied:
options: To the full dataset before any split
Only on the training split after train/test separation
On the test set to balance evaluation
Instead of collecting any real minority examples
items.answer: Only on the training split after train/test separation
items.explanation: Synthetic oversampling on test data leaks information; SMOTE belongs in training only.

items.question: On-the-fly augmentation means:
options: Pre-saving 10× augmented copies to disk offline
Applying random transforms when each training batch is loaded
Augmenting the test set once at deployment
Training without ever shuffling batches
items.answer: Applying random transforms when each training batch is loaded
items.explanation: Each epoch can see new random variants without storing every copy.
